algorithms.title: SALT Mass-Casualty Triage
algorithms.description: The CDC-sponsored national standard triage flow, from global sorting through category assignment.
steps: STEP 1 — Sort globally: 'If you can hear me and need help, walk to this area' (assess these third). 'Wave your arm or move' (assess these second). Still casualties or obvious life threats: assess FIRST.
STEP 2 — Assess each casualty individually, performing lifesaving interventions that take only seconds:
Open the airway by positioning; for a child, consider 2 rescue breaths.
Needle decompression for tension pneumothorax (trained providers).
Auto-injector antidotes if chemical exposure indicated.
Not breathing after airway opening (adult, or child after breaths) → DEAD (black).
Breathing: ask — obeys commands/purposeful movement? peripheral pulse? no respiratory distress? major hemorrhage controlled?
All YES + minor injuries → MINIMAL (green). All YES + significant injuries → DELAYED (yellow).
Any NO + survivable with current resources → IMMEDIATE (red). Any NO + survival unlikely with current resources → EXPECTANT (gray, comfort care).
Tag/mark, move to the next casualty, and RE-TRIAGE at the treatment area, before transport, and on arrival.
